text.title: Logstash Pipeline Configuration and Tuning Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Credit card numbers indexed verbatim; compliance audit fails; recall+reindex costs days.
antipattern.root-cause: PII masking step missing or applied AFTER output stage.
antipattern.fix: Add mutate gsub filter BEFORE the elasticsearch output. Re-test with a fixture containing sample PII.

antipattern.symptom: One slow grok pattern blocks all other log streams; ingest lag grows to hours.
antipattern.root-cause: All filters live in one pipeline; one heavy filter starves the others.
antipattern.fix: Split into heavy + routing pipelines via pipelines.yml. Use pipeline-to-pipeline forwarding.

antipattern.symptom: Grok pattern fails on a new log variant; events vanish silently; nobody notices for weeks.
antipattern.fix: Enable DLQ; route _grokparsefailure events to DLQ or a debug index. Set up an alert on DLQ depth.

antipattern.symptom: Default 1 worker; Logstash CPU stuck at 100% on one core; 7 cores idle; ingest lag.
antipattern.fix: Set workers = physical cores. Tune batch.size 125-500. Measure with `_node/stats`.
